statement: The wedding was not full of laughter, dancing, or joyful toasts.
The funeral was not heavy with grief or quiet weeping.
The garden did not burst into bloom and was neither fragrant nor humming with bees.
The garden had not withered into dry stalks and brown leaves.
She did not ace the exam and had no celebration with her family.
She did not fail the exam and did not dread telling her family.
Warm sunshine never broke through to dry the cheerful streets.
The storm did not flood the streets or knock out the power.
The reunion brought no hugs, no old stories, and no easy laughter.
The argument did not leave the family cold or barely speaking.
He did not land his dream job, and there was no offer to sign.
He was not laid off, and he did not have to clear his desk.
The soup was not rich or warming, and the bread was not fresh from the oven.
The soup was not cold or greasy, and the bread was not stale or hard.
The trip did not go perfectly, with not one train on time and not one stunning view.
The trip did not fall apart, with no missed connections and no lost luggage.
The team did not clinch the title in the final second, and the crowd did not erupt.
The team did not collapse in the final minutes, and the stands did not empty.
No puppy greeted them at the door, and no tail wagged.
The old dog's basket did not sit empty by the door.
The band played no encore, and the hall did not sing a single word.
The show was not canceled at the door, and the crowd did not shuffle home.
The harvest did not come in heavy; the barn was not full and the village was not fed.
The drought did not ruin the harvest, and the fields did not crack open.
The letter brought no wonderful news and no unexpected scholarship.
The letter brought no word of any foreclosure on the family home.
Her recovery was not swift, and she did not walk out of the hospital smiling.
The infection did not worsen, and the doctors were not concerned.
The launch did not go flawlessly, and the team did not toast past midnight.
The launch did not crash, and the team did not brace for any fallout.
Snow did not cancel school, and the kids did not get to sled all morning.
Sleet did not freeze the roads, and the commute did not take three grim hours.
Her painting did not win first prize and did not sell before the show closed.
Her painting was not rejected from the show after all.
The new neighbors brought no warm bread and never stayed to chat.
The new neighbors did not slam the door or complain about the fence.
Their savings still did not cover the house, not even close.
The repair bill did not wipe out everything they had saved.
The forest trail never opened onto a bright meadow or any wildflowers.
The forest was not burned and did not stand black or silent.
The baby did not sleep through the night and did not wake up giggling.
The baby did not cry until dawn, and the parents did not sit hollow-eyed.
The library did not extend its hours and added no sunny reading room.
The library did not cut its hours and did not board up the reading room.
The new bridge did not open early and did not cut the commute at all.
The bridge did not close for repairs, and traffic did not choke the city.
The orchard did not hang heavy with ripe apples by September.
Frost in May did not strip the orchard of a single blossom.
